rulings.Case Title: UNITED COMMERCIAL BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. MERIDIAN SHOPPING CENTER LLC, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 19, 2013, line 1. PLAINTIFF UNITED COMMERCIAL BANK's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. Moving party failed to shift burden. No competent evidence provided as to existence and contents of the Guarantee. Moving party failed to request judicial notice of the complaint and exhibits thereto. Moving party failed to provide a courtesy copy of the complaint. Exhibits in the complaint on file are not numbered and electronic version of complaint on file does not contain a document titled "Guarantee". = (501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay Of Administrative Decision Pending The Court'S Decision On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 19, 2013, line 3. PETITIONER DENISE BEATTY's Motion For Stay Of Administrative Decision Pending The Court's Decision On Petition For Writ Of Mandate DENIED. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DENISE BEATTY VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 19, 2013, line 3. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Petition is untimely as filed outside the 90 day jurisdictional limitation. Petitioner's reliance on Guevara for the proposition that statute was tolled is misplaced. 28 USC 1367 is not applicable. Petitioner fails to allege in complaint or state in opposition that she can allege facts in support of equitable tolling, specifically that respondent was a party to both actions; petitioner had timely notice of the UD action; and that petitioner's belief that UD filed by a third party is proper vehicle to initiate judicial review of respondentt's decision was reasonable. = (501/REQ)
